item.title: Coherent control of interacting solid-state spins below the diffraction limit
item.author: Haitong XuMehmet T. UysalJeff D. Thompson
item.content:encoded:
Interacting electron spins in solid-state devices could be used for quantum networks, but such spins are often too closely spaced to be individually addressed. Single-spin control has now been demonstrated for interacting rare-earth dopants.

Haitong XuMehmet T. UysalJeff D. Thompson

Nature Physics

item.title: Ultrashort light pulses can transform a semiconductor into a topological insulator
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
The electronic structure of materials can be tailored using pulses of light, and through this so-called Floquet engineering, novel materials can be designed. Now, the technique has been used to create light-induced metallic surface states in the band gap of a semiconductor — transforming the material, transiently, into a topological insulator.

Nature Physics

item.title: Floquet topological state induced by light-driven band inversion in SnTe
item.author: F. ChassotA. PulkkinenC. Monney
item.content:encoded:
Floquet engineering could, in principle, induce a topological phase in semiconductors, but clear evidence has been lacking. Now it is shown in SnTe that femtosecond light pulses briefly create a topological state that is absent at equilibrium.

F. ChassotA. PulkkinenC. Monney

Nature Physics

item.title: Deuterated compounds for organic light-emitting diodes
item.author: Ross D. Jansen-van VuurenJingwen YaoShou-Cheng Dong
item.content:encoded:
This Review surveys recent advances in deuterated compounds for light-emitting devices, addressing the advantages of deuteration, its working mechanisms, and prospects for wider implementation.

Ross D. Jansen-van VuurenJingwen YaoShou-Cheng Dong

Nature Photonics

item.title: Fourier pixels for bidirectional light control
item.author: Yannik M. GlauserSander J. W. VonkDavid J. Norris
item.content:encoded:
A versatile platform of miniaturized Fourier-optics-based diffractive elements enables multifunctional pixels that fully control and sense the amplitude, phase and polarization of optical wavefronts for advanced photonic applications.

Yannik M. GlauserSander J. W. VonkDavid J. Norris

Nature

item.title: Chiral laser gyroscopes breaking the lock-in limit
item.author: Yuan-Hao MaoJi-Peng XuHui Jing
item.content:encoded:
Ring laser gyroscope lock-in has been eliminated using spontaneous symmetry breaking in a He–Ne laser, enabling accurate near-zero rotation sensing without external components, improving miniaturization and precision.

Yuan-Hao MaoJi-Peng XuHui Jing

Nature

item.title: Optical cooling by interfacial charge transfer in 2D heterostructures
item.author: Jiamin LinBaixu XiangWeigao Xu
item.content:encoded:
Optical cooling in two-dimensional semiconductor heterostructures is demonstrated through phonon-assisted interfacial charge transfer, enabling cryogen-free thermal management without stringent quantum-efficiency requirements.

Jiamin LinBaixu XiangWeigao Xu

Nature

item.title: Unconditional advantage of quantum teleportation over direct transmission of single photons through a lossy channel
item.author: Li-Chao PengDian WuJian-Wei Pan
item.content:encoded:
Shared quantum entanglement can be used to transfer quantum states from one location to another. An experiment has now demonstrated this transfer with higher efficiency than directly sending single photons through a lossy channel.

Li-Chao PengDian WuJian-Wei Pan

Nature Physics

item.title: Emergent partons in fractional quantum Hall systems
item.author: Zihao YangYifan WangLingjie Du
item.content:encoded:
In a geometrical theory for the fractional quantum Hall effect, chiral spin-2 neutral excitations arise. Now, multiple such excitations are observed and provide evidence for the parton description of the quantum Hall effect.

Zihao YangYifan WangLingjie Du

Nature Physics

item.title: A photonic integrated long-distance quantum communication network
item.author: Likang ZhangJiawei PanJian-Wei Pan
item.content:encoded:
A four-user quantum spine-leaf network over up to 540-km fibre is demonstrated using a twin-field quantum key distribution protocol. It allows the full connections between any two user pairs and is operated at a 1.25 GHz clock rate with a secret key rate of 9.38 × 10−9 bit per pulse.

Likang ZhangJiawei PanJian-Wei Pan

Nature Photonics

item.title: A 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum computer with all-to-all connectivity
item.author: Anthony RansfordM. S. AllmanJustin G. Bohnet
item.content:encoded:
A new quantum computer, Quantinuum Helios, which is a 98-qubit trapped-ion quantum processor built on the QCCD architecture, demonstrates performance well beyond classical capabilities and provides a path for scaling up quantum computing.

Anthony RansfordM. S. AllmanJustin G. Bohnet

Nature

item.title: Reconfigurable quantum computer juggles 98 qubits
item.author: Crystal Noel
item.content:encoded:
A quantum computer based on trapped ions can connect any two quantum bits, reaching performance levels that conventional computers cannot match.

Crystal Noel

Nature

item.title: Spatial distribution of the proteome in the human body and in cancers
item.author: Liang YueWenhao JiangTiannan Guo
item.content:encoded:
A spatially resolved map of the human proteome across a variety of healthy tissues and cancers provides wide-ranging insights in developmental biology and oncology, and could aid the identification of therapeutic targets and development of treatments for cancer.

Liang YueWenhao JiangTiannan Guo

Nature

item.title: A prototype differential atom interferometer for fundamental physics
item.author: C. F. A. BaynhamR. HobsonM. G. D. van der Grinten
item.content:encoded:
A prototype differential atom interferometer operates at the standard quantum limit with no excess noise beyond atom shot noise, achieving performance in line with the specifications for future long-baseline atom interferometers.

C. F. A. BaynhamR. HobsonM. G. D. van der Grinten

Nature

item.title: Visualizing the impact of quenched disorder on 2D electron Wigner solids
item.author: Zhehao GeConor SmithMichael F. Crommie
item.content:encoded:
A technique combining atomically resolved scanning tunnelling microscopy with neural-quantum-state quantum Monte Carlo simulation of disordered 2D electron Wigner solids establishes a powerful framework to enable the clear identification of two distinct defect-induced disorder regimes.

Zhehao GeConor SmithMichael F. Crommie

Nature

item.title: Bigger and faster computation with photons
item.author: Lishu Wu
item.content:encoded:
Lishu Wu

Nature Physics

item.title: Strong coupling of a microwave photon to an electron on helium
item.author: Gerwin KoolstraElena O. GlenJohannes Pollanen
item.content:encoded:
Individual electrons on superfluid helium can be used as qubits. Strong coupling between the motional state of a single superfluid helium-bound electron and a single cavity photon has now been demonstrated, enabling qubit readout schemes.

Gerwin KoolstraElena O. GlenJohannes Pollanen

Nature Physics

item.title: Quantum Fisher information in a strange metal
item.author: Federico MazzaSounak BiswasSilke Paschen
item.content:encoded:
A strange metal is a strongly correlated quantum state that occurs in many different materials. Now, quantum Fisher information is shown to increase as the strange metal forms, indicating that the state has high multipartite entanglement.

Federico MazzaSounak BiswasSilke Paschen

Nature Physics

item.title: Tiny thermometers
item.author: Leonardo Benini
item.content:encoded:
Leonardo Benini

Nature Physics

item.title: A productive puzzle
item.author: N/A
item.content:encoded:
High-temperature superconductivity continues to be a crucial research topic in condensed matter physics. We consider the advances that it has inspired.

Nature Physics

item.title: Orbital Hall conductivity and relaxation in thin films with variable disorder
item.author: Min-Gu KangFederica NasrPietro Gambardella
item.content:encoded:
The role of disorder in orbital angular momentum transport induced by electric fields is not well understood. Now orbital Hall conductivity and orbital relaxation are shown to be robust even in strongly disordered thin films.

Min-Gu KangFederica NasrPietro Gambardella

Nature Physics

item.title: High-fidelity entanglement and coherent multi-qubit mapping in an atom array
item.author: Aruku SenooAlexander BaumgärtnerAdam M. Kaufman
item.content:encoded:
Ytterbium-171 atoms can store quantum information in different ways, with their own applications and advantages. Now the ability to coherently transfer entangled states between these different qubit types has been demonstrated.

Aruku SenooAlexander BaumgärtnerAdam M. Kaufman

Nature Physics

item.title: Logical qubits with erasure conversion using metastable neutral atoms
item.author: Bichen ZhangGenyue LiuJeff D. Thompson
item.content:encoded:
Qubits with detectable erasure errors can support more efficient error correction than qubits with unlocated errors. Improved logical qubit performance using erasure conversion has now been demonstrated using ytterbium-171 atoms.

Bichen ZhangGenyue LiuJeff D. Thompson

Nature Physics

item.title: Improved quantum processor logical error rates via correction and detection
item.author: A. PaetznickB. W. ReichardtK. M. Svore
item.content:encoded:
Experimental demonstration of quantum error-correcting codes combined with error detection and post-selection applied to a trapped-ion quantum processor shows improvements in logical error rates ranging from 11× to 800× compared with several physical circuit baselines.

A. PaetznickB. W. ReichardtK. M. Svore

Nature

item.title: Spinon mediation of witness spin dynamics in herbertsmithite
item.author: Hiroto TakahashiJack MurphyJ. C. Séamus Davis
item.content:encoded:
There is a long-running effort to find a material with a spin liquid ground state, but existing experimental probes have not given definitive evidence. Now, impurity spins have been used as probes of the putative spin liquid phase of herbertsmithite.

Hiroto TakahashiJack MurphyJ. C. Séamus Davis

Nature Physics

item.title: Two-component exciton condensates in an electron–hole bilayer
item.author: Ruishi QiQize LiFeng Wang
item.content:encoded:
Macroscopic quantum coherence arises in two-component exciton Bose–Einstein condensates within MoSe2/hBN/WSe2 electron–hole bilayers, exhibiting distinct spin–valley polarized phases, quantum phase transitions under magnetic fields and stable condensate behaviour up to approximately 1.8 K.

Ruishi QiQize LiFeng Wang

Nature

item.title: Lignin to adipic acid in a high-yield chemical and biological redox process
item.author: Kathryn M. MainsChad T. PalumboGregg T. Beckham
item.content:encoded:
A chemical and biological redox process that resembles processes in petrochemical refining is used to convert lignin from poplar into a single, valuable bioproduct, adipic acid, in high yields.

Kathryn M. MainsChad T. PalumboGregg T. Beckham

Nature

item.title: Observation of exact quantum critical states
item.author: Wenhui HuangXin-Chi ZhouDapeng Yu
item.content:encoded:
Anderson localization allows critical states between localized and extended regimes, but observing them is difficult. Now critical states are experimentally realized in a superconducting-qubit simulator implementing a quasiperiodic lattice model.

Wenhui HuangXin-Chi ZhouDapeng Yu

Nature Physics

item.title: The thin line for optical neural networks towards broad practical relevance
item.author: Anas SkalliDaniel Brunner
item.content:encoded:
Optical neural networks promise unmatched efficiency, bandwidth, and latency — critical benefits as demand for neural network hardware surges. However, their practical value for general-purpose acceleration or specialized applications must be proven under application-realistic conditions. We discuss recent insights and outline key research priorities.

Anas SkalliDaniel Brunner

Nature Photonics

item.title: Quantum coherent beating in polar disubphthalocyanine-fullerene cocrystals for ultrafast photothermal conversion
item.author: Yong ChenYu ZhangCheng Zhang
item.content:encoded:
Quantum coherent beating and ultrafast energy transfer have been observed in a polar disubphthalocyanine-fullerene cocrystal. The temperature reached 228 °C within 5 s following 0.5 W cm−2 laser irradiation, and an equilibrium temperature of 242 °C was achieved, which is higher than those of many other organic solids.

Yong ChenYu ZhangCheng Zhang

Nature Photonics

item.title: High-speed non-volatile barium titanate field-programmable photonic gate array
item.author: Cristina Catalá-LahozJose Roberto Rausell-CampoJosé Capmany
item.content:encoded:
Barium titanate ferroelectric is used in photonic phase shifters arranged in a hexagonal waveguide mesh, forming a programmable photonic array. The device achieves sub-100-ns switching speeds, while each individual gate retains its state without continuous powering, enhancing overall energy efficiency.

Cristina Catalá-LahozJose Roberto Rausell-CampoJosé Capmany

Nature Photonics

item.title: Microsoft upgrades controversial quantum chip — researchers are still sceptical
item.author: Davide Castelvecchi
item.content:encoded:
The tech giant says it will make ‘topological’ quantum computers that can be scaled up faster than competing technologies.

Davide Castelvecchi

Nature

item.title: Chiral superfluorescence from perovskite superlattices at room temperature
item.author: Qi WeiJonah S. PeterMingjie Li
item.content:encoded:
Chiral perovskite superlattices exhibit room-temperature circularly polarized superfluorescence, with emission intensity and polarization controllable by weak magnetic fields.

Qi WeiJonah S. PeterMingjie Li

Nature

item.title: Spermine is an endogenous iron chelator that inhibits ferroptosis
item.author: Man LiXuexin YuJun Li
item.content:encoded:
A non-canonical metabolic route for spermine synthesis mediated by ALDH18A1 limits iron availability and lipid peroxidation in hepatocellular carcinoma.

Man LiXuexin YuJun Li

Nature

item.title: Daily briefing: Bad supervisors bump early-career researchers out of academia
item.author: Flora Graham
item.content:encoded:
Almost 40% of respondents to a survey said that their supervisor was disorganized and a poor communicator. Plus, promising results against a hard-to-treat cancer and whether AI will help or hinder social sciences.

Flora Graham

Nature

item.title: Hong–Ou–Mandel interference of more than ten indistinguishable atoms
item.author: Martin QuensenMareike HetzelCarsten Klempt
item.content:encoded:
Hong–Ou–Mandel interference has been difficult to extend to ensembles of many massive particles. Measurements on up to 12 neutral atoms now exhibit characteristic multiparticle interference pattern with single-atom resolution and negligible loss.

Martin QuensenMareike HetzelCarsten Klempt

Nature Physics

item.title: Single- and two-mode magnon thermal squeezing
item.author: Tomosato HiokiKaito TojoEiji Saitoh
item.content:encoded:
Obtaining squeezed states in magnets are a key challenge for future spin-based sensing and information technology. Now thermal squeezing of magnons, where noise drops below thermal levels, is shown in a ferrimagnetic insulator device.

Tomosato HiokiKaito TojoEiji Saitoh

Nature Physics

item.title: Polymarket vs science: why researchers are sceptical of the prediction-market hype
item.author: Jenna Ahart
item.content:encoded:
Online prediction markets are taking bets on everything from climate change to quantum computing. But researchers question their accuracy.

Jenna Ahart

Nature

item.title: How jazz boosts my creativity in physics
item.author: Dom Byrne
item.content:encoded:
Stephon Alexander says his passion for musical improvisation helps him to understand and teach complex ideas.

Dom Byrne

Nature

item.title: Experimental randomness amplification
item.author: Anatoly KulikovSimon StorzRenato Renner
item.content:encoded:
An experimental realization of device-independent randomness amplification is demonstrated using superconducting qubits, in which a source of weak, correlated randomness is converted into virtually perfect random bits, certified by a Bell test.

Anatoly KulikovSimon StorzRenato Renner

Nature

item.title: Dynamical freezing for magnetometry in an interacting spin ensemble
item.author: Ya-Nan LuDong YuanPan-Yu Hou
item.content:encoded:
Dynamical freezing, a mechanism by which a driven quantum system may not thermalize to a featureless ‘infinite-temperature’ state at long times, is experimentally observed in an ensemble of interacting nitrogen-vacancy spins in diamond.

Ya-Nan LuDong YuanPan-Yu Hou

Nature

item.title: Exclusive: NSF puts new research grants to top universities on hold
item.author: Dan Garisto
item.content:encoded:
US science funder once again restricts awards to Harvard University and other institutions.

Dan Garisto

Nature

item.title: A direct black-hole mass measurement in a little red dot at high redshift
item.author: Ignas JuodžbalisCosimo MarconciniSaiyang Zhang
item.content:encoded:
A direct, dynamical black-hole mass measurement in a strongly lensed little red dot at high redshift indicates that it is a massive black-hole seed caught in its earliest accretion phase.

Ignas JuodžbalisCosimo MarconciniSaiyang Zhang

Nature

item.title: Robustness of Majorana modes to a disorder potential in atomic chains on a superconducting Rashba alloy
item.author: Harim JangDaniel CrawfordRoland Wiesendanger
item.content:encoded:
The presence of Majorana zero modes is often difficult to confirm experimentally due to the detrimental effects of disorder. Now, a chain of magnetic atoms on a proximatized surface is shown to exhibit robust Majorana states with disorder present.

Harim JangDaniel CrawfordRoland Wiesendanger

Nature Physics

item.title: Bottom-up synthesis of molecular nanodiamond from nanographene
item.author: Jiaxu LiangChristopher P. EnderTanja Weil
item.content:encoded:
Bottom-up synthesis from molecular nanographenes produces ultrasmall, uniform nanodiamonds for quantum colour centre incorporation, enabling scalable fabrication of high-quality quantum sensing materials.

Jiaxu LiangChristopher P. EnderTanja Weil

Nature

item.title: An on-chip programmable valley optoelectronic nanocircuit
item.author: Chi LiKaijian XingHaoran Ren
item.content:encoded:
An on-chip light-programmable valley optoelectronic nanocircuit enabled by meta-waveguide photodetectors integrated with transition metal dichalcogenides is demonstrated. In situ generation, selective routing and electrical readout of valley-dependent chiral photons are achieved.

Chi LiKaijian XingHaoran Ren

Nature Photonics

item.title: Nonlinear atomic tunnelling boosted by bright squeezed vacuum
item.author: Zhejun JiangShengzhe PanJian Wu
item.content:encoded:
Bright squeezed vacuum light boosts nonlinear atomic tunnelling ionization more than 20-fold compared with coherent light, enabling quantum control of strong-field processes without increasing classical intensity.

Zhejun JiangShengzhe PanJian Wu

Nature

item.title: Quantum light source boosts attosecond science
item.author: Jan Marcus Dahlström
item.content:encoded:
Ionization experiment shows that quantum light can behave like a conventional laser that has a higher intensity.

Jan Marcus Dahlström

Nature

item.title: The CAPTCHA protocol
item.author: Shiao Wang
item.content:encoded:
Difficult choices.

Shiao Wang

Nature

item.title: A deep-learning framework reveals whole-body perturbations at cell level
item.author: Doris KalteneckerIzabela HorvathAli Ertürk
item.content:encoded:
An analysis of diet-induced obesity using MouseMapper—a suite of foundation-model-based deep-learning algorithms—identifies structural alterations of the infraorbital branch of the trigeminal ganglia.

Doris KalteneckerIzabela HorvathAli Ertürk

Nature
